title: DIY Stackable Water Bottle Storage Rack
summary: A shallow two-panel rack with three 1x2 rails that cradles water bottles on their sides, sized to whatever length fits your cabinet and stackable with mounting tape.
description:
This is a build-to-fit pattern rather than a single fixed size: pick the length that fits your cabinet or drawer first, then cut the three 1x2 rails to that length minus 1-1/2" — the example below uses a 12" rack that holds about three or four standard bottles, but the same steps scale to whatever width you need. The two outer 1x2 rails stand on edge as raised side walls that keep bottles from rolling off, while the center 1x2 lies flat as a bottom support between the two end panels.

Build several and stack them with mounting tape (or similar) for a taller pantry or cabinet system — each rack is a complete, independent unit, so there's no interlocking joinery to align between layers.

materials.name: 1-5/8" trim screws or 1-1/4" brad nails and wood glue
Mounting tape
materials.note: Only needed if stacking multiple racks.

cutList.note: 12" rack shown — cut to your own desired rack length minus 1-1/2".

steps.title: Cut the pieces
steps.body: Decide how long your water bottle organizer will be and cut the three 1x2 rails to that length minus 1-1/2". Cut two 1x6 end panels at 8" long. Sand any rough cut edges.

steps.title: Assemble the rack
steps.body: Screw or nail the 1x6 end panels to the 1x2 rails. One screw per joint is enough if screwing; if nailing, use glue and two nails per joint. The outer 1x2 rails stand vertically as side walls; the center 1x2 lies flat.
materials: 1-5/8" trim screws or 1-1/4" brad nails and wood glue

steps.title: Stack if desired
steps.body: If building multiple racks to stack, use mounting tape to hold them together.

images.alt: A small stackable wood rack holding water bottles on their sides
